summary: Large header/prototype cleanup pass to support building with Metrowerks `-requireprotos`. The PR added many missing function prototypes and forward declarations across MSL, Runtime, Dolphin SDK subsystems, sysdolphin/baselib, fighter, stage, item, and game-mode code; moved callback typedefs into forward headers where needed; and adjusted several C function definitions to match explicit prototypes, especially low-level Dolphin functions such as PPCArch and CARD routines. Evidence is mainly the title, changed-file list, and diff excerpt; there was no PR body or review-comment text in the slice.
what_changed: Added new MSL/Runtime headers for previously unprototyped library functions, including `critical_regions.gamecube.h`, `abort_exit.h`, `console_io.h`, `ctype.h`, `string.h`, `Gecko_setjmp.h`, `__init_cpp_exceptions.h`, and `global_destructor_chain.h`.
Expanded existing MSL headers with prototypes such as `__prep_buffer`, `__flush_buffer`, and `fwide`, and included dependencies like `<stdio.h>` where needed for `FILE`.

Introduced subsystem forward headers, notably `src/dolphin/os/forward.h`, `src/dolphin/card/forward.h`, `src/dolphin/dvd/forward.h`, `src/dolphin/gx/forward.h`, and `src/sysdolphin/baselib/forward.h`, to break type-dependency cycles while still satisfying required prototypes.
Moved or centralized callback/type declarations such as `CARDCallback` into forward headers instead of defining them only in umbrella headers like `src/dolphin/card.h`.

Added many missing Dolphin SDK prototypes across CARD, DVD, DSP, DB, GX, OS, and PAD headers; examples in the excerpt include `CARDFormatAsync`, `CARDDeleteAsync`, `CARDGetStatus`, `CARDProbe`, `DVDGetCommandBlockStatus`, `DVDCancelAsync`, `DVDGetCurrentDiskID`, and `DSPAssertTask`.

Adjusted C definitions to match explicit prototype types. In `CARDBios.c`, several `s32 chan` parameters were changed to `EXIChannel chan`, including `__CARDEnableInterrupt`, `__CARDEraseSector`, and `__CARDGetControlBlock`.
Adjusted low-level inline/asm PPCArch function signatures from implicit `void` forms to typed signatures, e.g. `PPCMfmsr` and `PPCMfl2cr` returning `u32`, and register-write functions taking `u32`.
Removed local ad-hoc declarations once real headers existed, e.g. `CARDMount.c` no longer needs a local `__CARDUnlockedHandler` declaration.

Restructured `dvdqueue.c` to include its own header first and to type the queue nodes around `DVDCommandBlock`; the excerpt shows replacement of `_DVDQueue_sub` with `DVDNode` and a `WAITING_BLOCK` cast macro.

Added many fighter and melee subsystem headers containing prototypes, including Donkey, Samus, Koopa, MasterHand, Mario, Yoshi, IceClimber, Link, Luigi, Peach, Purin, Seak, `ftbosslib.h`, `ftparts.h`, `mpcoll.h`, `ifstatus.h`, `gmmain_lib.h`, and `ground.h`.
Removed or reduced some obsolete declarations and headers, including deletion of `src/dolphin/gx/GX_unknown_001.h` according to the changed-file list.
smart_moves: Used forward headers for shared opaque or callback types instead of forcing large umbrella includes everywhere; this is important for `-requireprotos` because every call needs declarations but not every translation unit needs full struct definitions.
Matched function definitions to header prototypes rather than only adding declarations, reducing signature drift between C files and headers.

Preserved decomp-friendly SDK-style organization by creating per-module headers such as `CARDDelete.h`, `CARDFormat.h`, `CARDStat.h`, and `CARDWrite.h` rather than dumping all prototypes into a single global header.
Removed local one-off forward declarations after adding canonical prototypes, making future mismatches easier to catch under `-requireprotos`.
Added prototypes incrementally across many small headers, which lets unrelated decomp work include only the narrow module interface it needs.
For asm functions, made return and argument types explicit at the function definition site, which is necessary because `-requireprotos` exposes otherwise tolerated implicit or mismatched signatures.
decomp_lessons: When enabling `-requireprotos`, expect broad churn: every cross-TU call, runtime helper, SDK callback, and asm stub needs a visible declaration before use.
Prefer canonical headers over local `extern` or in-file declarations; local declarations can hide signature drift and make later matching work brittle.
Create lightweight `forward.h` headers for typedefs, callback aliases, and opaque structs that are needed by multiple subsystem headers. This reduces include cycles and keeps prototypes visible.
Keep implementation signatures synchronized with headers immediately; adding prototypes alone can reveal incorrect parameter types such as `s32` versus enum/typedef aliases like `EXIChannel`.
Library/runtime headers should still include `Runtime/platform.h` or other base type providers so prototypes using `s32`, `u32`, `bool`, or SDK aliases compile in isolation.
For decomp projects, empty or near-empty per-module headers can still be useful scaffolding when they establish include guards and a canonical location for future prototypes.
Asm functions should not be left as `asm void` if callers rely on a return value; encode the real ABI-level return type, usually `u32` for register reads.
Header cleanup PRs can matter even without changing matching logic because they allow stricter compiler flags to catch missing or stale declarations.
naming_conventions: Header include guards follow path-derived uppercase names such as `MSL_ABORT_EXIT_H`, `RUNTIME_GECKO_SETJMP_H`, `DOLPHIN_CARD_CARDFORMAT_H`, and `DOLPHIN_CARD_FORWARD_H`.

Dolphin SDK internal functions retain double-underscore prefixes, e.g. `__CARDEnableInterrupt`, `__CARDGetControlBlock`, `__CARDUnlockedHandler`, `__flush_buffer`, `__setjmp`, and `__destroy_global_chain`.
Per-module Dolphin headers are named after SDK source modules, e.g. `CARDBios.h`, `CARDMount.h`, `CARDFormat.h`, `CARDDelete.h`, `CARDStat.h`, `CARDRdwr.h`, and `CARDUnlock.h`.

Forward declaration headers use the simple name `forward.h` within each subsystem directory, e.g. `dolphin/card/forward.h`, `dolphin/dvd/forward.h`, `dolphin/os/forward.h`, and `dolphin/gx/forward.h`.
Callback typedefs preserve SDK-style names such as `CARDCallback` and `DVDCBCallback`.
Fighter headers follow existing character/module naming such as `ftdonkey_SpecialN.h`, `ftsamus3.h`, `ftkoopa.h`, and `ftMasterHand_30.h`.

The diff includes some weak/placeholder names such as `func_80325F18`, `DSPSomething`, and `DummyLen`; these appear evidence-grounded but likely represent unresolved symbol naming rather than final semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Explicitly typed PPC special-register helpers in `PPCArch.c`: read helpers like `PPCMfmsr`, `PPCMfl2cr`, and `PPCMfhid2` return `u32`; write helpers like `PPCMtmsr`, `PPCMtl2cr`, `PPCMthid2`, and `PPCMtwpar` take `u32`.
Kept `asm` definitions under `MWERKS_GEKKO` and matching non-Gekko `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` stubs, but synchronized both branches' signatures so headers and definitions agree.

Used forward-declared queue/callback types to avoid needing complete structs before prototypes; e.g. DVD callback and command block declarations are split into `dolphin/dvd/forward.h` and used by `dvd.h`/`dvdqueue.c`.

In `dvdqueue.c`, the queue storage is typed as a small node struct whose fields match `DVDCommandBlock` linkage, with a cast macro `WAITING_BLOCK(idx)` to treat queue sentinels as command blocks; this is a decomp tactic for matching SDK intrusive-list layouts while keeping types explicit.
Changed CARD routines from raw `s32 chan` to `EXIChannel chan` where the SDK channel type was intended, preserving ABI-compatible integer passing while improving prototype correctness.
follow_up_queries: Find pass 1 of `Support -requireprotos` to understand the staged strategy and any conventions introduced before PR 698.
Search later PRs for cleanup of duplicate prototypes visible in this diff excerpt, such as repeated declarations in `CARDBlock.h`, `CARDCheck.h`, `CARDDir.h`, and `CARDOpen.h`.
Search for later renames of weak placeholder symbols introduced or exposed here, including `func_80325F18`, `DSPSomething`, `DummyLen`, and `GX_unknown_001`.
Check whether all newly created empty scaffolding headers such as `CARDCreate.h`, `CARDRename.h`, and `CARDWrite.h` later gained real prototypes.
Search for build-system changes that actually enable `-requireprotos` after this header pass.
